author: @bartronicsecurity
text: I’ve been following you play with magnets and enjoying your findings. When you put a piece of steel against a magnet it will naturally weaken the magnetic flux as the flux is now contained in the “Keeper” piece of steel. But if the piece of steel is not large enough to fully contain the magnetic field there will be a small amount of residual field still around the magnet. So instead of nothing on the piece of steel there is some magnetism now which can be strengthened to produce more magnetism by a coil with current going through it. The current needed to produce a strong magnetic field will be less than that needed by a pure electromagnet as it is assisted by the rare earth magnet. The whole concept you are playing with is in essence I see it as a way to putting a magnetic bias on that piece of steel. Nothing more really. This concept is used in electronics. As an analogy put a bias of say 1,5 volt above ground using a single cell results in a standing voltage of 1,5 volt cell from ground. Add another 1,5 volt cell to it and it increases the voltage to 3 volts (higher voltage). But you can also put a reverse 1,5 volt and you get 0 volts (analogy being reverse the magnetic field to get zero magnetism). The trick is being able to use this knowledge in some way to create a useful device. But don’t think of a perpetual motion motor. This does not exist.

author: @benthere8051
text:
You can "bias" any electromagnet with a permanent magnet to make the resulting magnet stronger. That is what you did in your video. 
And just as important, you can "turn off" any permanent magnet with an electromagnet whose field is the reverse of that of the permanent magnet.
I've found the second instance to be the more useful of the two. The zero-power permanent magnet can hold an armature in place and a short pulse of energy into an electromagnet can release the armature. 
I used that effect as a brake on a drum. A drum was held in place by a permanent magnet and the drum was allowed to spin when that field was canceled by an electromagnet. The quiescent state was ZERO-POWER. There were no complicated mechanical latches or moving parts involved when the brake was on. The brake was disengaged when the field was on, and there were no moving parts.
